caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 4-digit 5418 is an agency-heavy proxy rather than a clean 541890 staffing census.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The role mix is reinterpreted toward the promo-products dominant-subsegment hypothesis; demonstration/event firms would have lower automation.
inputs.pi_dist: 4imprint is a large direct operator, not a lower-middle-market independent.
inputs.pi_moat: No source in this packet measures account portability for 541890 targets.
inputs.t50_years: The observed 50% figure is only for extra-large distributors and measures use, not effective workflow adoption.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not a directly observed full-industry or NAICS adoption rate.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is an analogue, not a quantified historical study.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Unobserved estimate; do not treat as a demographic fact.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of this packet's evidence is not proof of no shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: Announcements do not show acquisition cadence, target size, or willingness to buy every small distributor.
inputs.buy_mult: No disclosed 541890 lower-middle-market transaction multiple was reopened; the cited category is an adjacent proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: Expansion requires demonstrated retention; recent acquisition announcements disclose no transaction multiples.
inputs.pricing_structure: The full catch-all code includes service models that differ from promo distribution.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $189,523 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541890); class <5: 4,615 firms, avg revenue/firm $481,015 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $75,279 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 806 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,794,295 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $280,807 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 442 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,905,484 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $611,208 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 398 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,154,884 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,902,239 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 84 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,233,821 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,296,593 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 52 firms, avg revenue/firm $112,013,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,530,113 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 482

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Automation is concentrated in creative, quoting and order operations; physical demos and relationship selling remain. The supplied 5418 occupation proxy is agency-contaminated, so fractions are bounded judgments, not observed occupation measurements.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541890 ($4,995,994,780) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541890 ($19,434,299,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact artifact identity, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. All nine registered sources were reopened; the score-driving sources support the limited claims made, and the packet openly treats promotional-products distribution, adoption, role mix and valuation as scoped proxies or estimates. The low-confidence kill thesis remains useful with all 13 authored caveats and no additional material or validator-authored defect.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 541890
sources.what_it_supports: Official NAICS context: 541890 is the residual advertising-services industry after named advertising categories are excluded.

sources.title: NAPCS product list for NAICS 54189
sources.what_it_supports: The 541890 product list includes coordinating production and delivery of logoed advertising specialties and merchandise demonstrations.

sources.title: ASI Releases 2025 Counselor State of the Industry
sources.what_it_supports: ASI characterizes the promotional-products industry as $26.6bn and bases its State of the Industry on distributor and supplier surveys.

sources.title: ASI 2025 Counselor State of the Industry release
sources.what_it_supports: The release reports that 50% of extra-large distributors with $5m+ sales use AI and says creative copy is the most common use among AI users.

sources.title: 4imprint Financial Highlights
sources.what_it_supports: A scaled North American promotional-products distributor reported 2025 revenue, gross profit and operating profit, illustrating a viable direct-distribution model.

sources.title: HALO Acquires What's Happening Promotions
sources.what_it_supports: HALO announced its October 2025 acquisition of a promotional marketing agency.

sources.title: BDA acquires Harper + Scott
sources.what_it_supports: BDA announced its September 2025 acquisition of a creative branded-merchandise agency.

sources.title: Overture Promotions acquires Pride Products
sources.what_it_supports: Overture announced its January 2026 acquisition of a promotional-products distributor.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry, 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Its Q1 2026 observations show a 4.0x–6.5x $3m–$10m EBITDA range for marketing/digital agencies and identify retention and specialization as valuation drivers.

title: Other Services Related to Advertising
